LAST_DONE:    Sub-batch 5.2a (materializePatrolTrack helper + ER client fetchSubjectTracks) SHIPPED to main via squash-merge as d2a1dd1. 6 files changed +910 / -39. New: packages/jobs/src/lib/__tests__/patrol-track-materialization.test.ts (~516 LOC, 14 vitest cases). Relocated: apps/web/src/server/sync/patrol-track-materialization.ts (35 LOC stub) → packages/jobs/src/lib/patrol-track-materialization.ts (301 LOC real impl) via git mv. Modified: packages/jobs/src/lib/earthranger-client.ts (+81 LOC — added ErTrackCoordinate3 + ErTrackFeature + ErTrackResponse interfaces; extended constructor with optional `trackToken?: string` arg defaulting to main `token`; refactored private `request<T>(path, bearer?)` to accept override bearer; added `fetchSubjectTracks(subjectId, since, until): Promise<ErTrackResponse>` hitting `/api/v1.0/subject/{id}/tracks/?since=&until=` using trackToken when set); packages/jobs/src/index.ts (+13 LOC re-exports materializePatrolTrack + MaterializationResult + MaterializationSkipReason; PrismaClientLike NOT re-exported because it would collide with area-derivation re-export — same alias to ExtendedPrismaClient); packages/jobs/package.json (+1 LOC subpath export `./lib/patrol-track-materialization`). Helper signature: `materializePatrolTrack(prisma: ExtendedPrismaClient, patrolId: string): Promise<MaterializationResult>` where `MaterializationResult = {patrolTrackId: string|null, pointCount: number, hasTimestamps: boolean, lastTrackTime: Date|null, patrolEnded: boolean, skipped: boolean, skipReason?: "no_segment"|"no_leader"|"no_credentials"}`. Loads patrol+first segment via Prisma select with `orderBy:{scheduledStart:"asc"}, take:1` for deterministic single-segment selection. Three early-return skip paths (no exception): no_segment (patrol has no segments OR pickTimeRange returns null), no_leader (segment[0].leaderErId is null), no_credentials (tenant lacks earthrangerUrl or earthrangerDasToken). Tenant credentials decrypted via @marine-guardian/db.decrypt; earthrangerTrackToken preferred when present (passed to EarthRangerClient constructor as optional 3rd arg). Time range fallback order: segment.actualStart/End → scheduledStart/End → patrol.startTime/endTime → new Date() (open patrol fetches up to now). Feature summary: pointCount sums coordinates across all LineString features; hasTimestamps is DEFENSIVELY false when ANY feature has missing or length-mismatched coordinateProperties.times (partial response should not silently lie about temporal coverage); lastTrackTime is max parseable timestamp, null when hasTimestamps=false. Atomic upsert on patrolId unique constraint with source="er_api", fetchedAt=now, full GeoJSON FeatureCollection in trackGeojson (Prisma Json column). Two errors caught + fixed before merge: (1) ESLint @typescript-eslint/no-unnecessary-type-assertion on `as PatrolForMaterialization` after Prisma select narrowing — fixed by typed binding `const patrol: PatrolForMaterialization = await ...`; (2) `makeTenant({ earthrangerUrl: null })` override silently resolved to default because `null ?? "enc:..."` evaluates to default with nullish coalescing — two `no_credentials` test cases failed initially. Fixed by switching makeTenant to `in`-operator pattern. Logged 🟡 fix entry to lessons.md as recurring pattern for any test factory accepting nullable Partial<T> overrides. Verification (on feat/5.2a-patrol-track-materialization branch AND independently re-verified on main after squash-merge): jobs lint 0/0 errors+warnings, jobs typecheck 0 errors, jobs tests 71/71 PASS (was 57 baseline + 14 net new = 71 total — matches expected delta), web typecheck 0 errors (cross-package import surface intact via @marine-guardian/jobs root barrel), web lint 0/0 errors+warnings (--max-warnings 0), web tests 281/281 PASS unchanged (5.2a is jobs-only — zero web changes). Total monorepo: 338 → 352 (+14). Branch feat/5.2a-patrol-track-materialization single commit b38d951 → squash-merged to main as d2a1dd1 → branch force-deleted via `git branch -D` per Rule 23 standard cleanup (content preserved in d2a1dd1). Dispatch path: main-session Opus 4.7 implementation per STATE.md 5.1e/5.1d post-mortem recommendation. Dispatch-mechanism issue from 5.1d (2 consecutive Opus subagent stalls returning interim status with `:agentId:` continuation hint mid-stream) has NOT been root-caused or resolved — so main-session remains the safe fallback for cleanly-scoped sub-batches under ~50K actual tokens. 5.2a consumed ~80K cumulative including orientation + verification + governance — within main-session SAFE zone via tight read discipline (Read with explicit offsets + grep instead of full-file reads). At session start: 9 prior commits (6687112 4.1e + 0cf7eeb 4.1e-governance + 6686042 5.1a + fccd56e 5.1a-governance + c4a14a9 5.1b + f9eaafe 5.1b-governance + 3b4145f 5.1c + 91d6389 5.1c-governance + 3bb4794 5.1c-lessons + 068d32f 5.1d-B + 225d66a 5.1d-governance + 88744ed 5.1e + af7cfd7 5.1e-governance) were pushed to origin/main bringing remote current up through af7cfd7 — user explicitly authorized via AskUserQuestion at session start. Origin/main is now 1 implementation commit behind local (d2a1dd1 5.2a) + 1 pending governance commit.

LAST_DONE:    Sub-batch 5.1d Area B (AreaBoundary CUD fan-out) SHIPPED to main via squash-merge as 068d32f. 2 files changed, +241 / -4. Modified: apps/web/src/server/trpc/routers/areaBoundary.ts (+57 LOC — `fanOutAreaRederive(tenantId, userId): Promise<{enqueued: number}>` private helper; queries Event + Patrol + FuelEntry via Promise.all with explicit `where: { tenantId }` defense-in-depth scoping per security.md; enqueueAreaRederive per row with entity casing "event"|"patrol"|"fuelEntry" matching AreaDerivationEntity contract from 5.1b helper; userId threaded from ctx.userId since adminProcedure guarantees it and BaseJobPayload requires it; wired into create unconditionally, into update + delete gated on `result.count > 0` to avoid spurious fan-outs on no-op writes; return shape extended from raw Prisma result to `{boundary|result, fanOut}`); apps/web/src/server/trpc/routers/__tests__/areaBoundary.test.ts (+188 LOC — extended prisma mock with event/patrol/fuelEntry findMany; mocked @marine-guardian/jobs for enqueueAreaRederive; updated existing create test assertion from `result.id` → `result.boundary.id` per new return shape; +6 new test cases in dedicated describe block: (1) create fans out for every Event+Patrol+FuelEntry with correct payload shape per entity, (2) create fan-out queries scoped to requesting tenant with explicit tenantId where clauses, (3) update fans out when result.count > 0, (4) update does NOT fan out when result.count === 0 (no findMany calls either), (5) delete fans out when result.count > 0, (6) delete does NOT fan out when result.count === 0). Initial typecheck flagged that BaseJobPayload requires `userId` (interface line 3) — fixed by threading ctx.userId through fanOutAreaRederive signature and updating all 5 enqueueAreaRederive calls; tests updated to assert userId in payload. Verification (both on feat/5.1d-area-boundary-cud-fanout branch AND independently re-verified on main after squash-merge): web lint 0/0 errors+warnings, web typecheck 0 errors, web tests 274/274 PASS (was 268 baseline + 6 net new = matches expected delta), jobs lint+typecheck clean, jobs tests 57/57 unchanged (5.1d-B is web-only). Total monorepo test count 325 → 331 (+6). Branch feat/5.1d-area-boundary-cud-fanout single commit bbd1bee → squash-merged to main as 068d32f → branch force-deleted per Rule 23 standard cleanup. NO AuditLog write — deferred to 5.1e per Option A scope split (admin manual-rebuild has full ctx.session.userId context). NO jobs package changes — 5.1d-B is consumer-side wiring only; the queue + processor + worker built in 5.1c handle the producer/consumer flow unchanged. Scope adjustment from original brief: Area A (er-sync inline re-derive on areaName change — v2 L546) deferred. Original brief assumed sync upserts compare incoming vs persisted areaName and call applyAreaDerivation on change, but ErEvent/ErPatrol interfaces in packages/jobs/src/lib/earthranger-client.ts do not currently include area_name, AND the syncEvents/syncPatrols data spreads in er-sync.processor.ts do not map any area_name field. v2 L456 says area_name should be "preserved verbatim from ER" — so Area A's precondition is an unbuilt sync-engine feature (area_name ingestion). Implementing Area A would require either bootstrapping that ingestion (out of scope for 5.1d as scoped) or implementing a phantom feature that never fires because sync never sees areaName changes. User confirmed Ship Area B Only path via AskUserQuestion. Dispatch path experience for this sub-batch: 2 consecutive Opus 4.7 subagent dispatches (ab538b0b5337b5cae, abbccff0dd9aba1b8) returned what reads as interim status messages mid-stream ("Reading the queue and area-rederive worker to verify enqueueAreaRederive signature, and verifying jobs barrel exports:" followed by ":agentId:" continuation hint; "Noted — skill suggestion ignored per [[feedback_skill_injection_hooks_ignore_when_locked_stack]]. Tech stack is locked in inputs.yml (priority 6) which overrides skill suggestions (priority 7). Continuing." followed by ":agentId:" continuation hint). Both burned ~70s + ~10 tool uses each (~287K + ~264K total_tokens reported) with zero git state changes — verified via git log/status/branch. SendMessage tool not surfaced in inventory (ToolSearch "select:SendMessage" returned "No matching deferred tools found" twice), so neither agent could be continued. User chose main-session Opus 4.7 implementation as the unblock; entire 5.1d-B work landed cleanly in ~10 minutes including governance. The dispatch-mechanism issue is the only open question — could be (a) framework-level Agent output budget that returned interim narration as terminal output, (b) PreToolUse hook noise (skill injection) that the executor interpreted as a stop signal, or (c) some Opus 4.7 subagent behavior where the model returns at natural sentence boundaries when uncertain. Worth investigating before next dispatch — but main-session is a safe fallback for cleanly-scoped sub-batches under ~30K main-session tokens.

Reason: 5.1d sync engine processor lives in @marine-guardian/jobs and apps→packages dependency direction is one-way (apps depend on packages, packages NEVER depend on apps); 5.1e admin tRPC import surface becomes `import { applyAreaDerivation } from "@marine-guardian/jobs"` via root barrel re-export. Total monorepo test count unchanged at 325 (268 web + 57 jobs vs prior 276 + 35 + 14 = same total). Branch feat/5.1c-area-rederive-queue built across 2 commits (8f094f7 implementation + 8cb3d3f registration wiring) → squash-merged to main as 3b4145f → branch force-deleted with `git branch -D` per Rule 23 standard squash-merge cleanup (content preserved in 3b4145f, direct ancestry check fails after squash so -D required — same pattern as 5.1a + 5.1b). Dispatch experience: single Opus 4.7 executor (via Agent(model: "opus")) reported STATUS: DONE_WITH_CONCERNS with 6 reasonable concerns: (1) helper relocation apps/web→packages/jobs architecturally required not optional — 5.1d processor in jobs needs helper, apps→packages is one-way; (2) added @marine-guardian/shared workspace dep — required by relocated helper's deriveArea import; (3) `platformPrisma as unknown as ExtendedPrismaClient` cast with explanatory comment — type-narrowing decision documented inline so 5.1d processor (next sub-batch) inherits the pattern; (4) base-worker.ts extended with optional limiter field, backward-compatible; (5) two commits collapsed via squash-merge as standard; (6) AREA_REDERIVE_CONCURRENCY=10 chosen as conservative default — v2 L545 specifies limiter not concurrency, reasoning: limiter is binding constraint (50 jobs/sec ceiling) so concurrency just prevents Redis fetch latency bottleneck. All 6 concerns sound; architect accepted without modification. Trust-but-verify pass (git log + diff stats + independent lint/typecheck/test rerun on main) confirmed accuracy. Token usage ~358K cumulative for executor across 67 tool uses (parent session pays full cost via Agent boundary on return); main-session reasoning stayed targeted via STATE.md NEXT-field driven dispatch — no full PRODUCT.md / v2 spec / governance doc reads needed because Item 1 contract is fully encoded in STATE.md ledger after 3 sub-batches. Continues [[feedback_marine_guardian_opus_executor_default]] doctrine — Opus executor on Marine-Guardian remains the right default after this third consecutive clean dispatch.

NEXT:         🎯 USER RESUME ENTRYPOINT: Open a FRESH Claude Code session and say "Start Sub-batch 5.2b" (or "Continue Batch 5 Item 2"). 🎯 Item 2 progress: 1 of 3 sub-batches shipped (5.2a). Next concrete task is **Sub-batch 5.2b — BullMQ patrol-track-materialize queue + processor + worker**. Brief shape: (a) New `packages/jobs/src/queues/patrol-track-materialize.queue.ts` (Queue + `PatrolTrackMaterializeJobPayload` type with `{tenantId, userId, patrolId}` + `enqueuePatrolTrackMaterialize(payload): Promise<string>` helper; jobId pattern `patrol-track-materialize:${tenantId}:${patrolId}` for BullMQ-level dedupe at enqueue time so two rapid enqueues for the same patrol collapse to one job); (b) New `packages/jobs/src/processors/patrol-track-materialize.processor.ts` (thin wrapper around materializePatrolTrack helper — same platformPrisma→ExtendedPrismaClient cast pattern as 5.1c area-rederive.processor.ts per [[2026-05-20 🟡 platformPrisma → ExtendedPrismaClient cast pattern for BullMQ processors]]; threads job.data.patrolId into helper; returns MaterializationResult for BullMQ result storage so 5.2c admin UI can surface skipReason if any); (c) New `packages/jobs/src/workers/patrol-track-materialize.worker.ts` (factory `startPatrolTrackMaterializeWorker()` using base-worker.createWorker with conservative concurrency + rate limiter sized for ER API budget — recommend starting at concurrency=5, limiter={max:20, duration:1000} which is more conservative than 5.1c's 50/sec because ER's tracks endpoint typically has stricter rate limits than the events/patrols endpoints; lock these in DECISIONS_LOG.md inline with the sub-batch); (d) Modify barrels: queues/types.ts (+PatrolTrackMaterializeJobPayload + QUEUE_NAMES.PATROL_TRACK_MATERIALIZE='patrol-track-materialize' + JobPayloadMap entry), queues/index.ts, processors/index.ts, workers/index.ts (+startPatrolTrackMaterializeWorker + concurrency/limiter constants), start-workers.ts (+register as 6th worker), packages/jobs/src/index.ts (+re-exports). Test coverage target: queue 5-6 cases + processor 6-8 cases + worker 4-5 cases = ~15-19 net new tests, bringing jobs to ~90 total. Estimated ~25-30K main-session actual including orientation + verification. Producer side wired ONLY via 5.2c admin tRPC mutation in the near term — er-sync.processor inline enqueue DEFERRED per [[2026-05-20 🟡 Test helper overrides null nullish coalescing]] (no — wrong link) per Area A deferral pattern. **Sub-batch 5.2c queued after 5.2b**: `patrol.rebuildTracks` adminProcedure tRPC mutation on patrol router. User selected tenant-wide fan-out via AskUserQuestion at session start (same shape as 5.1e areaBoundary.rebuild): adminProcedure, optional tenantId for super_admin cross-tenant (omit/match own for site_admin), enqueues materialize per active Patrol in tenant via the 5.2b queue, AuditLog with PLATFORM:PATROL_TRACK_REBUILD vs PATROL_TRACK_REBUILD per security.md superadmin convention, returns {tenantId, enqueued, action}. Minimal UI stub button on the existing patrols list page (apps/web/src/app/(dashboard)/patrols/page.tsx) — same shadcn/ui Dialog + useSession role-gating pattern as 5.1e's RebuildAreaBoundariesButton. Estimated ~20K. **DEFERRED — er-sync.processor inline enqueue**: adding `enqueuePatrolTrackMaterialize` after each successful patrol upsert in syncPatrols. Parallel to 5.1d Area A deferral pattern: no tests exist for er-sync.processor.ts yet, and adding a hot-path enqueue without test coverage would be undertested. Pickup path: a precursor sub-batch bootstraps er-sync.processor.test.ts harness (significant — ~15-25K tokens itself), then includes the inline enqueue cleanly. This also unblocks 5.1d Area A — both can share the same test infra investment. After Item 2 ships (5.2a ✅ + 5.2b + 5.2c), Item 3 (ReportExport pdf-render queue + Puppeteer Docker service + streaming download Route Handler) → then Batch 5 COMPLETE → proceed to Batch 6+ (breaking migrations + user-visible features). See [[project_marine_guardian_batch5_queued]] memory entry for full Batch 5 plan.

DEFERRED:     5.1d Area A (er-sync inline re-derive on areaName change per v2 L546). Precondition unmet: ErEvent/ErPatrol payload types do not currently include `area_name`, and syncEvents/syncPatrols data spreads do not map any area_name field. v2 L456 says area_name should be "preserved verbatim from ER" but that ingestion has not been built. Two paths to pick up later: (1) precursor sub-batch 5.1d.A extends ErEvent + ErPatrol interfaces to include `area_name?: string | null`, adds `areaName: e.area_name ?? null` to syncEvents data spread + `areaName: p.area_name ?? null` to syncPatrols upsert data, bootstraps er-sync.processor.test.ts (no tests exist for this processor yet — significant test infrastructure work), then adds inline-derive logic; (2) fold Area A into a future Item that owns area_name ingestion as an explicit deliverable. Architect's call on which path. Either way, Area A is unblocked once area_name flows through sync.
